RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per CCP 36(d). Trial is May 28, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Plaintiff stipulates that summary motions may be heard on 28 days notice without an additional two days for e-service. Last day for hearing summary motions is May 16, 2013, with oppositions due 14 days before the hearing and replies due 7 days before the hearing. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than May 3, 2013. Defendants to respond no later than May 10. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on May 16, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 10, 2013. Expert discovery cut off is pursuant to C.C.P. No later than Feb. 22, 2013, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than Feb, 22, 2013, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further Case Management Conference on May 8, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference on May 28, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 503 with the Court pursuant to S. F. Local Rules of Court 5.0. Parties are required to submit statements by noon on May 23. Plaintiffs' and defendants' representatives fully knowledgeable about the case and with authorities to settle must be present, unless the Court determines, prior to the settlement conference, that good cause exists to have that person/persons appear by telephone. The parties must contact Pang Ly by 5/23 for such request. Plaintiff should provide all bankruptcy claim forms to all defendants within 2 weeks of submission all claims to bankruptcy trusts, and within 7 court days afterwards. All parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Order signed after the hearing. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
